primary_source.title: WebDriver BiDi
primary_source.relevant_normative_shape: Local-end messages are CommandResponse, ErrorResponse, or Event; ErrorResponse.id may be a non-negative integer or null.

finding: A verifier that consumes raw WebSocket frames needs a direction-aware normalization boundary before semantic transcript validation. Null-id protocol errors must be represented as uncorrelated failures rather than crashing or being silently discarded, and client command objects must not be accepted on the server-message stream.
design_implication: Browser adapters should record raw server-to-client frames, pass them through normalizeBidiServerFrames, and only pass an accepted normalized transcript to the existing mc.webdriver-bidi-verification.v2 validator.

cases: valid event and success response accepted
client command on server stream rejected
null-id protocol error preserved and rejected

claim_states.observed: The W3C message grammar separates server messages into success responses, error responses, and events, and permits null for an error-response id.
claim_states.inferred: A direction-aware raw-frame normalizer reduces ambiguity and adapter-specific transcript fabrication.
claim_states.proposed: Use this normalizer as the first stage of Cloudflare and Vercel BiDi evidence capture.
claim_states.superseded: Passing arbitrary parsed JSON directly to the semantic transcript validator.
claim_states.unresolved: No live browser WebSocket transcript has yet been captured and normalized.

evidence_strength: primary specification plus deterministic adversarial unit tests
falsification_route: Capture raw server-to-client frames from a current conforming BiDi implementation. Revise the schema if a valid server message cannot be represented without information loss or if the normalizer accepts a message forbidden by the current grammar.
limits: WebDriver BiDi remains a Working Draft
normalization does not prove that client commands were emitted
normalization does not prove browser conformance
normalization does not prove deployment provenance or claim truth
privacy_boundary: Contains only public specification references, repository paths, commit identifiers, abstract protocol claims, and synthetic test data.
